title: DEFAULT Constraint
content:
### DEFAULT Constraint

The DEFAULT constraint provides a default value for a column when no value is specified in an INSERT statement.

---

### DEFAULT Syntax

```sql
CREATE TABLE employees (
    id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(100),
    hire_date DATE DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    salary DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0.00,
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    department VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'General'
);
```

### Common Default Values

| Data Type | Common Default |
|-----------|---------------|
| Integer | 0 |
| Decimal | 0.00 |
| String | '' (empty string) or 'N/A' |
| Date | CURRENT_DATE or CURRENT_TIMESTAMP |
| Boolean | FALSE or TRUE |
| UUID | GEN_RANDOM_UUID() or NEWID() |

### Adding DEFAULT to Existing Table

```sql
ALTER TABLE employees
ALTER COLUMN status SET DEFAULT 'active';
-- SQL Server
ALTER TABLE employees ADD CONSTRAINT df_status DEFAULT 'active' FOR status;
-- MySQL
ALTER TABLE employees ALTER status SET DEFAULT 'active';
```

### Dropping DEFAULT

```sql
ALTER TABLE employees ALTER COLUMN DROP DEFAULT;
-- MySQL
ALTER TABLE employees ALTER status DROP DEFAULT;
-- SQL Server
ALTER TABLE employees DROP CONSTRAINT df_status;
```

### DEFAULT and INSERT

```sql
INSERT INTO employees (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Alice');
-- hire_date defaults to CURRENT_DATE
-- salary defaults to 0.00
-- active defaults to TRUE

-- Explicit override of default
INSERT INTO employees (id, name, salary) VALUES (2, 'Bob', 50000);
```

> **Key Point:** DEFAULT values save time by automatically populating columns when no value is provided. They do not affect queries — you can still specify explicit values.

quiz.question: When does a DEFAULT value apply?
options: Always, even when a value is provided
Only when no value is specified for that column in INSERT
